description: In 2010, Apple supplier Foxconn faced intense global scrutiny following a series of worker suicides at its Shenzhen facility. This sparked a massive debate on the ethics of global supply chains, tech manufacturing, and corporate responsibility, leading to immediate changes in labor policies and mandatory audits.

key_events.description: International media reports emerge detailing horrific working conditions and high suicide rates.
Public outcry peaks as NGOs organize global protests outside Apple retail stores.
Foxconn announces massive wage hikes and the installation of anti-suicide nets, shifting the narrative.
Apple joins the Fair Labor Association to increase oversight, stabilizing brand reputation.

calibration_notes: Useful for modeling high-impact moral panics that start with low awareness and evolve into structural corporate changes.
